name: when-the-air-hits-your-brain
description: Frank Vertosick's memoir of neurosurgery training — facing irreversible decisions, learning through failure, and maintaining humanity under extreme pressure.
useCase: High-stakes decision making, recovering from costly mistakes, emotional survival under pressure, patient-first humanity, and enduring brutal apprenticeships.
knownRisks: The book is a memoir from the 1990s — some medical practices have changed significantly since
Graphic medical descriptions may be distressing to some readers
The 'surgical psychopath' framework is metaphorical, not a clinical diagnosis
